title: Knowledge is Money
content: Helped someone buy their first car. She was terrified of dealers. We spent two weeks researching. Invoice prices, trade-in values, competing offers. She walked into that dealership knowing more than the salesperson. Got $3,200 under asking. Knowledge is literally money.
extended_content: She came to me shaking. 'I'm going to get ripped off. I know it.' I said, 'Not on my watch.' We built a spreadsheet. Every car she was considering - invoice price, dealer cost, current incentives, what people were actually paying in her area. We practiced negotiation scenarios. By the time she walked in, she had three competing quotes and knew exactly what the car was worth. The salesperson's tricks didn't work because she'd already heard them all. She texted me from the parking lot: 'I DID IT.' Best text I ever got.
lesson: Research is the best negotiation tool
